user_query:
I run a small community bakery that trains formerly incarcerated people. I noticed odd login activity on our training portal and need a quick, local risk review and a staff-friendly notice. Please work entirely in this workspace using the provided files. Deliverables and constraints:

1) Build and run a command-line analysis script (e.g., Python) named scripts/analyze_log.py that reads:
- input/auth_log.csv
- input/staff_roles.csv

Requirements for the analysis:
- Treat all rows as events; compute overall totals, but focus failure metrics only on rows where event=login and status=failure.
- Aggregate outputs:
  a) reports/failed_summary.csv with columns: ip, total_failures, unique_users, first_seen (ISO8601), last_seen (ISO8601), peak_60min_count, flagged. Flag an IP as true if it generates more than 5 login failures within any rolling 60-minute window (across any users).
  b) reports/user_failures.csv with columns: username, role (from staff file), active (yes/no from staff file), failures, last_failure_date (YYYY-MM-DD). Include users with at least one login failure.
  c) reports/overall_stats.json with fields: total_events, total_login_failures, unique_ips_with_failures, top_ips_by_failures (list of {ip,count} for the top 3), per_hour_failure_mean, per_hour_failure_max. Compute hourly stats over the hours present in input/auth_log.csv.
- Make the script runnable from the shell. When executed, it must print a single summary line to stdout: "Analyzed N events; X failures; Y flagged IPs" (substitute numbers).
- Run the script from the command line and capture both the exact command you ran and the one-line stdout summary into reports/run.log.

2) Rewrite the notice in input/draft_notice.txt into memo/security_notice.txt:
- Keep it under 180 words.
- Friendly, supportive tone suitable for staff and trainees; no blame or alarmist language.
- Clearly ask folks to: enable 2FA, use strong passphrases, and report suspicious emails or login prompts. Include a note that a short refresher will be offered this week.

Assumptions:
- No Internet access is needed.
- You may choose any implementation language, but the above output paths and file names must match exactly.

Deliverables to produce:
- scripts/analyze_log.py (the CLI program)
- reports/failed_summary.csv
- reports/user_failures.csv
- reports/overall_stats.json
- reports/run.log (the command used and the printed summary line)
- memo/security_notice.txt All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
